phase_name: Mainline capability-parity implementation after architecture slimming

completed_tasks.name: Audit current capability surface and GhostCore reuse points
completed_tasks.progress: Read the required docs and knowledgebase files, mapped the currently implemented endpoints (/tree, /find, /tap, /swipe, /type), confirmed LocalApiServer/StateCoordinator/GhostCoreAccessibilityService as the main integration points, and identified the requested parity gaps.

remaining_tasks.name: Deliver the planning-only report with next stage recommendation

blockers.description: No technical blocker was found for the planning task; the turn was interrupted before the staged parity plan was written up.
blockers.workaround: Resume from the gathered audit evidence and write the five-part planning report only.

blockers.description: The user also asked for service-button toggle behavior and a small alignment cleanup on the current UI, but that is separate from the planning-only capability-parity task.
blockers.workaround: Keep those UI fixes as a follow-up after the parity plan is delivered unless the user reprioritizes them again.

decisions.decision: Keep Ghosthand accessibility-first and root optional/secondary.
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly reset the architecture and does not want a separate root execution direction.

decisions.decision: Use the proven external reference implementation patterns as the basis for parity work, but keep Ghosthand naming, API shape, and architecture.
decisions.rationale: The user wants direct port/adaptation rather than re-deriving accessibility mechanics.

decisions.decision: Planning must come before the next coding pass for the parity program.
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly asked for a staged implementation plan only in this task.

decisions.decision: The dark premium UI refinement was completed and verified on-device, but UI work should stop here until the mainline parity plan is reported.
decisions.rationale: The latest user instruction made capability parity the highest-priority mainline task.

next_action: Start by converting the gathered audit into the requested five-part planning report: current phase, parity gap list, staged implementation plan (Stage 1-3), reusable Ghosthand components, and the first stage to execute next.
context_notes: Latest verified state before pausing: the dark premium operator surface is live on the device, the default home surface no longer shows foreground package, and no backend behavior was changed. During the new high-priority parity-planning task, the codebase audit confirmed that LocalApiServer currently exposes /tree, /find, /tap, /swipe, /type, plus /health, /state, /device, /foreground, /launch, /stop, and that GhostCoreAccessibilityService already contains reusable gesture and focused-text primitives. The staged parity plan itself was not yet written when the user interrupted.
